description: Jv t ft I Ic Volume VIII Terrible Battle Off the Philip pine Islands SPANIARDS SUFFER DEFEAT Gunboat Austria Blown Up and Two Cruisers Burned Total Rout of the Enemy by Admiral DeweyAmericans Show Gnat Skill and Courage While Engaged In the fightGive Addititioiml Luster to History of Navy MADRID May 4Via Bayonne New York World CablegramI am now able to give the first consecu tive account of the greatest naval battle since the ironclad was invented the greatest naval fight and the most over whelming naval victory in the annals of the United States The sailing of the American fleet from Hong Kong April 27 was promptly cabled to Manila and in spite of the authorities it was soon known through out the island Many of the better class at once hurried aboard merchant vessels with their valuables and fled The Spanish fleet which the governor general had been overpersuaded by Admiral Montejo to put to sea and meet and destroy the cowardly Yankee COMMODOBK DEWET I pigs there was recalled Saturday afternoon and lined up seven milea down the bay from Manila at Cavite where the arsenals dry dock and naval works are defended by a long line
